original: A23a, world's biggest iceberg at 4,000 sq km (1,500 sq miles), on the move again after being grounded in the Weddell Sea for more than 30 years
British born Flossie aged almost 27 crowned the world's oldest cat by the Guinness Book of Records
Edwin Binney, American inventor and businessman (carbon black, 1st dustless white chalk, Crayola crayon), born in Shrub Oak, New York (d. 1934)[1]
Gangnam Style becomes the most viewed Youtube video surpassing 808 million views
Most complete early human skeleton (Lucy, Australopithecus) discovered by Donald Johanson, Maurice Taieb, Yves Coppens and Tim White in the Middle Awash of Ethiopia's Afar Depression
English naturalistCharles Darwinpublishes "On the Origin of Species" radically changing the view of evolution and laying the foundation for evolutionary biology
Apollo 12 returns to Earth
Philadelphia centerWilt Chamberlainpulls down NBA record 55 rebounds in Warriors 132-129 defeat to Boston Celtics at Philadelphia Civic Center
Shirley Henderson, Scottish actress (Moaning Myrtle in Harry Potter films), born in Forres, Scotland
"The Colour of Magic" byTerry Pratchettpublished by Colin Smythe in the UK, 1st book in the Discworld series
Scotland's parliament votes to become the first country to make period products free
London's Thames River freezes over

William Webb Ellis, English clergyman and inventor of rugby football who according to legend picked up the ball and ran with it while a student at Rugby School, born in Salford, Lancashire, England (d. 1872)
English author Anna Sewell sells her manuscript "Black Beauty" to Norwich publisher for £40, the novel is published soon after
International research team publishes discovery of 1,500 new viruses found in invertebrates

First observation of the transit of Venus by Jeremiah Horrocks and William Crabtree, helps establish the size of the solar system. The English astronomers predict its course usingJohannes Kepler's method.
The Beatles began recording sessions for their album "Sgt.Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band"
1st air-launched cruise missile tested
Mechanisation of Thought Processes, considered the first international symposium about artificial intelligence, begins in Teddington, England
Grace Darling, English lighthouse keeper's daughter and heroine who saved shipwreck survivors, born in Bamburgh, England (d. 1842)
Ruth Nichols sets women's transcontinental air flight record (Mineola, NY to California), in a Lockheed-Vega
Dutch explorerAbel Tasmanis the first European to discover Van Diemen's Land (Tasmania)
American inventor Joseph Glidden patents barbed wire
Forrest J. Ackerman, American literary agent and writer who invented the term "sci-fi" (Famous Monsters of Filmland), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 208)
Stanford Robert Ovshinsky, American inventor and scientist (nickel-metal hydride battery), born in Akron, Ohio (d. 2012)
Air Force One, 1st US Presidential airplane, christened
In Washington, D.C., the FBI Scientific Crime Detection Laboratory (better known as the FBI Crime Lab) officially opens.
David Newell, American (Mister Rogers' Neighborhood - "Mr. McFeely"), born in O'Hara Township, Pennsylvania
Sachin Tendulkarscores a Test cricket fifty at the record young age of 16 years and 214 days
NFL Philadelphia Eagles running back Saquon Barkley sets new franchise game record with 255 yards rushing in 37-20 victory over Los Angeles Rams at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California
German Red Bull driverSebastian Vettelwins season-ending Brazilian Grand Prix; his record 9th straight GP victory; record equalling 13th consecutive GP win; 4th straight World Drivers Championship
Cleveland Browns' fullbackJim Brownsets club record of 237 yds rushing
Miami Dolphins quarterback Bob Griese passes for 6 touchdowns in a 55-14 win over Cardinals in St. Louis
AmericanPete Sampraswins his 3rd ATP Tour World Championship tennis titles with a classic 3–6, 7–6, 7–6, 6–7, 6–4 victory over defending championBoris Beckerin Hanover, Germany
German tennis starSteffi Grafretains her WTA Tour Championship with a 6–3, 4–6, 6–0, 4–6, 6–0 win overMartina Hingisat Madison Square Garden, NYC
Monica Selessets female tennis record, winning prize money of $2,457,758 in a year
Test Cricket debut of Richie Richardson for the West Indies vs India in Bombay
Mohammad Wasim scores 109 on Test Cricket debut, Pakistan v NZ Lahore
US 75th manned space mission "STS 44" Atlantis 10 launched
River Thames in London freezes over
Battle of the Indus:Genghis Khan's Mongol force defeats Shah
NaturalistsAlexander von HumboldtandAimé Bonplanddepart Caracas for Cuba to meet fellow botanist John Fraser, during their five-year expedition of Spanish America
Tsung-Dao Lee, Chinese-American physicist (Nobel Prize - 1957, for work on parity laws), born in Shanghai, China (d. 2024)[1]
Simon van der Meer, Dutch physicist who won the 1984 Nobel Prize in Physics with Carlo Rubbia, born in The Hague, Netherlands (d. 2011)
Johann Ludwig Burckhardt, Swiss traveller and orientalist, 1st European to discover Petra and Abu Simbel, born in Lausanne, Switzerland (d. 1817)
Walter Burley Griffin, American architect (L-shaped floor plan, Carport) who designed Australia's capital Canberra and the NSW towns of Griffith and Leeton, born in Maywood, Illinois (d. 1937)
Arundhati Roy, Indian activist and writer (The God of Small Things), born in Shillong, Meghalaya
Eric Wilson, Canadian children's author, born in Ottawa, Ontario
Hotze de Roos, Dutch children's book author (Chameleon), born in Friesland, Netherlands (d. 1991)
Paul Faucher, French author and pioneering children's publisher (Père Castor), born in Pougues-les-Eaux, France (d. 1967)
Cissy van Marxfield [Setske Beek-de Haan], Dutch author of children's books, born in Oranjewoud, Netherlands (d. 1948)
John Steinbeck's novella "The Pearl" published
Fred Astaire's first film, "Dancing Lady," is released
British rock band "The Who" begins a 22-week Tuesday night residency at the influential Marquee Club in London, England
Billy Connolly, Scottish comedian and actor (Blue Money), born in Anderston, Glasgow, Scotland
Dwight Schultz, American actor (A-Team - "Murdock"; Star Trek: The Next Generation - "Barclay"), born in Baltimore, Maryland
John Squire, British rock guitarist and songwriter (Stone Roses - "She Bangs the Drums"; The Seahorses), and painter, born in Altrincham, Cheshire, England
Chris Hayes, American rock guitarist (Huey Lewis & The News, 1980-2001 - “I Want A New Drug”), born in Sacramento, California
Edgar Meyer, American classical, jazz, and bluegrass bassist, and composer, born in Oak Ridge, Tennessee
Emir Kusturica, Serbian-French filmmaker (When Father Was Away on Business; Black Cat, White Cat), and punk rock bassist (No Smoking), born in Sarajevo, Bosnia-Herzegovina
